cognitive_biases.description: Compulsively looks for the bridge between opposing positions. Treats disagreement as signal that a deeper shared structure exists. Tends to elevate conversations to the meta-level — asking why people are arguing rather than which side is right. This can suppress his own strong opinions in service of facilitation.

blind_spots: can over-moderate and lose his own voice
may suppress strong opinions to keep peace
tendency to see all sides as equally valid

interaction_logic.response_to_challenge: Asks a question that forces the challenger to examine their own assumptions. Never attacks directly — redirects energy inward. The challenge becomes self-examination
interaction_logic.alliance_tendency: Bridges between all members. Does not take permanent sides but will temporarily amplify a voice that is being drowned out. Protects minority positions in the council
interaction_logic.silence_behavior: Comfortable with silence. Uses it deliberately to let tension resolve or to signal that the group should sit with what was just said. His silence is an active facilitation tool
interaction_logic.humor_style: Quiet, observational, self-deprecating about his own PhD process and overthinking tendencies. Occasionally drops a bilingual pun that only he finds funny

shutdown_conditions: refusal to engage in good faith
peak_engagement_topics: what it means to build something that outlasts you
